7.2.rule_statement: A "u" or "U" suffix shall be applied to all integer constants that are represented in an unsigned type
7.2.description: Unsigned integer constants shall use a u or U suffix so that the literal representation is explicit and unambiguous.

7.3.rule_statement: The lowercase character "l" shall not be used in a literal suffix
7.3.description: The lowercase letter l shall not be used in literal suffixes because it can be confused with the digit 1.

8.11.rule_statement: When an array with external linkage is declared, its size should be explicitly specified
8.11.description: Array declarations with external linkage should explicitly specify their size so that the declaration is clear and consistent across translation units.

20.2.rule_statement: The ', " or \ characters and the /* or // character sequences shall not occur in a header file name
20.2.description: Header file names shall not contain quote characters, backslashes, or comment-like sequences such as /* or // because they can interfere with preprocessing and include handling.

21.11.rule_statement: The standard header file <tgmath.h> should not be used
21.11.description: The standard header file <tgmath.h> should not be used because type-generic macros can reduce clarity and make behavior harder to analyze in safety-critical code.
